Option A says "totally reasonable" to deny someone based on skin color. Option B says "totally ridiculous" for the same action. Sarcasm usually involves saying the opposite of what is intended. So if the actual sentiment is against denying someone because of their skin color, then saying it's "reasonable" would be sarcastic. Conversely, if the speaker actually supports that denial, they might say "ridiculous" in a sincere way. But in real-life contexts, denying someone a chance due to skin color is widely considered wrong.
